title: Editorial: Recognizing Atypical Depression in Youth: Advancing Precision Diagnosis.
abstract: Depression is a growing public health crisis, affecting millions worldwide. Recent data indicate that 13% of individuals aged 12 and older experience depression, with the prevalence highest in adolescents aged 12 to 19 years.<sup>1</sup> Its marked heterogeneity-spanning symptoms, clinical courses, and biological mechanisms-drives variability in treatment response. Addressing this challenge requires identifying precise phenotypes or biologically informed subtypes. The Research Domain Criteria (RDoC) framework, developed by the National Institute of Mental Health, offers a strategy to define such subtypes, revealing distinct biological signatures and differential treatment responses and advancing the promise of precision psychiatry.<sup>2-4</sup>.
publication: Journal of the American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry
